name: GR 38: Wine and Fish Route Loop
name_original: Lagunas de Laguardia – circular desde Elciego
description: Cycle the moderate 16.4-mile Lagunas de Laguardia loop from Elciego, exploring vineyards, wetlands, and historic towns in Rioja Alavesa.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Elciego traverse the Rioja Alavesa wine region, characterized by extensive vineyards and rolling hills. The area is situated between the Sierra Cantabria mountains and the Ebro River, offering varied terrain for cyclists. Routes often incorporate the Mayor River and wetlands, providing diverse natural experiences. This geographical setting provides a mix of gentle slopes and more challenging climbs. Best touring cycling routes around Elciego The most popular touring cycling route is GR 38: Wine and Fish Route Loop, a 16.4 miles (26.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 57 minutes to complete. This route winds through vineyards, wetlands, and historic towns in Rioja Alavesa. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Church of San Andrés, Elciego – Puerto de Herrera (1,100 m) loop from Elciego, a difficult 32.7 miles (52.6 km) path. This route offers significant elevation gain and spectacular views of the Ebro Valley. Local touring cyclists also love the Elciego Wineries – Marqués de Riscal Hotel loop from Elciego, a 17.9 miles (28.8 km) trail leading through vineyards and past the iconic Bodega Marqués de Riscal, often completed in about 2 hours 6 minutes. Touring cycling around Elciego is defined by vast vineyards, rolling hills, and views of the Sierra Cantabria and Ebro River. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging climbs. The routes in Elciego are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 28 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Elciego's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Elciego?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes around Elciego, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult trails to explore the region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Elciego?
A: Touring cycling routes in Elciego primarily traverse the picturesque Rioja Alavesa wine region, characterized by extensive vineyards and rolling hills. The area is situated between the Sierra Cantabria mountains and the Ebro River, offering varied terrain from gentle slopes to more challenging climbs. You'll also encounter routes along the Mayor River and through wetlands, providing diverse natural experiences.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Elciego?
A: Yes, Elciego offers routes suitable for beginners and families. For an easy and scenic ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6953448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marqués de Riscal Hotel – Church of San Andrés, Elciego loop from Elciego&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.2 km long and takes about 1 hour 15 minutes. This route offers gentle gradients and pleasant views.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5343691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Andrés, Elciego – Puerto de Herrera (1,100 m) loop from Elciego&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 52.6 km path with significant elevation gain, offering spectacular views of the Ebro Valley from Puerto de Herrera.

Q: What natural features can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Elciego's touring cycling routes showcase a rich variety of natural features. You'll primarily ride through vast vineyards, but also encounter wetlands, especially around the Lagunas de Laguardia. The routes offer views of the Sierra Cantabria mountains and the Ebro River valley. You can also explore highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5150732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Prao de la Paul&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4450860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Carralogroño&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions to visit while cycling?
A: Yes, Elciego's routes pass by several significant landmarks. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14946299" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elciego Wineries – Marqués de Riscal Hotel loop from Elciego&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you past the iconic Bodega Marqués de Riscal, designed by Frank Gehry. You can also cycle to nearby walled medieval towns like Laguardia, or enjoy panoramic views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2850737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Herrera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Elciego?
A: The touring cycling routes around Elciego are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 28 reviews. Cyclists particularly praise the stunning vineyard landscapes, the varied terrain that caters to different skill levels, and the opportunity to combine cycling with cultural experiences like visiting wineries and historic towns.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Elciego?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Elciego are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068596335/gr-38-wine-and-fish-route-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 38: Wine and Fish Route Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 26.4 km circular trail that winds through vineyards, wetlands, and historic towns.

Q: Can I find routes that include views of the famous Marqués de Riscal Hotel?
A: Yes, there are specific routes designed to take you past the architectural marvel of the Marqués de Riscal Hotel. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14946299" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elciego Wineries – Marqués de Riscal Hotel loop from Elciego&lt;/a&gt; is a 28.8 km trail that leads directly through vineyards and past this iconic landmark.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Elciego?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Elciego is generally during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, ideal for cycling through the vineyards, and the landscapes are particularly beautiful with spring blossoms or autumn colors.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that explore the wetlands mentioned in the region?
A: Yes, the region offers routes that allow you to explore its unique wetlands. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068596335/gr-38-wine-and-fish-route-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 38: Wine and Fish Route Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, as it specifically winds through wetlands in addition to vineyards and historic towns in Rioja Alavesa.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that offer views of the Cliffs of Lapuebla de Labarca?
A: Yes, you can find routes that provide views of the impressive Cliffs of Lapuebla de Labarca. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33878538" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elciego Wineries – Cliffs of Lapuebla de Labarca loop from Elciego&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 15.9 km route that will take you through vineyards and past these notable cliffs.

Between 1200 BC and 250 BC this town was the most important in the area we know today as Rioja Alavesa. The town was structured into streets and squares and was the most populated in the area.

In addition to the excavations of the archaeological site, there is a small museum.

In the set of wetlands of Laguardia is the Prao de la Paul Lagoon, one of the four lagoons that make up this ecosystem. Surrounded by vineyards, this artificial lagoon has recently been drained to prevent the spread of invasive species, but it remains an important breeding ground for various birds. The wetland has a bird observatory.
With views of vineyards, wetlands and the Sierra Cantabria

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this cycling route from Elciego?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for Elciego are not provided, as a starting point for a popular cycling route in a wine region, you can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the town. Look for public parking areas or consult local signage upon arrival in Elciego.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features a mixed terrain. While much of the Rioja Alavesa area has well-paved surfaces, this specific trail includes segments that may be unpaved, with sections of stone and dirt track merged with asphalted road. Cyclists should be prepared for varied surfaces, potentially including gravel or dirt paths, which might require good tire clearance and handling skills.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'moderate' and requires 'good fitness'. While the shorter, self-contained Lagunas de Laguardia loop is considered easier, the full circular route from Elciego is longer (approximately 26.4 km) and has more elevation gain (around 290 meters), plus mixed terrain. It's not ideal for absolute beginners but suitable for those with some cycling experience and a reasonable fitness level.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll be immersed in the stunning landscape of Rioja Alavesa, with vast vineyards and panoramic views, often backed by the Sierra Cantabria mountains. Key highlights include the Prao de la Paul Lagoon and the broader Laguardia Lagoons wetland complex, a haven for diverse bird species. You'll also pass by the impressive Bodegas Ysios and the historic Town of La Hoya, with views of the charming medieval town of Laguardia.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Rioja Alavesa region is beautiful throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant landscapes (vineyards in bloom or changing colors). Summer can be hot, especially midday, while winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially less predictable weather.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Lagunas de Laguardia circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: While the Lagunas de Laguardia are a Protected Biotope, general cycling routes in the area typically allow dogs, especially if they are well-behaved and kept on a leash. However, specific rules for cycling with dogs on the unpaved sections or within the immediate vicinity of the lagoons might apply. Always ensure your dog is under control and respect any local signage regarding wildlife protection.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or to pay an entrance fee to cycle the Lagunas de Laguardia circular route. The route uses public roads and trails. However, as the Lagunas de Laguardia are a Protected Biotope, please respect the natural environment and any specific regulations posted in the wetland areas.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other trails. You will find sections that overlap with the Ardoaren eta arrainaren bidea, Oyón - Laguardia (GR-38), and Laguardia - Lagrán (GR-38), among others. This offers opportunities to explore further if you wish to extend your ride.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The Lagunas de Laguardia wetland complex is a Protected Biotope and a Ramsar site, making it a significant spot for birdwatching. You can expect to see a diverse range of bird species, especially migratory birds, as the lagoons serve as crucial resting and breeding grounds. Keep an eye out for various waterfowl and other wetland birds.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the Lagunas de Laguardia circular route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Elciego, which has various amenities. The charming medieval town of Laguardia, which you will pass near, also offers numerous options for cafes, restaurants, and even subterranean wineries. These towns are excellent places to stop for refreshments or a meal.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, meaning it's a loop trail. You will start and end in Elciego, completing a full circuit around the Lagunas de Laguardia and the surrounding Rioja Alavesa landscape.
